text.title: django-api Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-api as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Fat view: business logic (DB queries, balance checks, email sending) written directly in the view's post() method.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Fat views cannot be unit-tested without an HTTP request. Business logic in views is also unreachable from management commands, Celery tasks, or other entry points.

failure-mode.description: Business logic placed in serializer validate_* methods instead of a service function.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Serializers should validate shape and type only. Logic in validate_* is hard to test in isolation and creates hidden coupling between validation and business rules.

failure-mode.description: Using Meta.fields = "__all__" on a user-facing serializer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: New model fields (passwords, tokens, internal IDs) are automatically exposed when the model changes. An explicit allowlist is the only safe approach.

failure-mode.description: Using Serializer(data=request.data, partial=True) for PATCH without a separate UpdateSerializer.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reusing the create serializer with partial=True allows required fields to be cleared — violating invariants. A separate Update serializer makes all fields optional explicitly.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The browsable API changes content negotiation behavior (returns HTML when Accept: */* is sent), turns CSRF into a surprise vector, and leaks API structure to unauthenticated users.

failure-mode.description: Writing async def methods in plain DRF views without adrf — the method runs synchronously.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DRF's dispatch pipeline is synchronous. An async def view handler is called in a sync context; it appears to work but blocks the event loop in ASGI deployments.

failure-mode.description: Adding URLPathVersioning to REST_FRAMEWORK settings without adding <version> to the URL pattern.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The versioning class is applied but request.version is None — no error, silent failure. Tests must assert request.version is populated, not just that the endpoint responds.
